THE COURT SEVERS THE FEE SPLITTING PROVISION FROM THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENTS, FINDS THEM UNCONSCIONABLE, BUT OTHERWISE FINDS THAT THE AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE WITH THIS PROVISION SEVERED AND PROPERLY ADJUDICATED IN ARBITRATION. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Continued To Jul-25-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BIJOU - CENTURY, LLC's and Joseph Carouba's Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant Bijou - Century, LLC and Joseph Carauba's motion to compel arbitration is granted. The Court severs the fee splitting provision from the arbitration agreements, finds them unconscionable, but otherwise finds that the agreement is enforceable with this provision severed and properly adjudicated in arbitration. Under California law, there is a strong policy favoring arbitration and requiring close scrutiny of any claims of waiver. A party faces a heavy burden to establish waiver. (Saint Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195-1196; Cione v. Foresters Equity Services, Inc. (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 625, 638.) Here, the plaintiff has not met her burden to show that defendants' limited discovery constitutes a waiver of arbitration. The issue of unconscionability of the agreements is for the court to decide because the 2013 and 2014 agreements do not contain delegation clauses that clearly and unambiguously delegate this issue to the arbitrator. (Tiri v. Lucky Chances, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 231, 242.) It is unclear whether subsequent agreements cure this issue. The Court finds that the agreements' fee splitting provisions are substantively unconscionable. (McManus v. CIBC World Markets Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 76, 93.) With that provision severed and unenforceable, the remaining provisions of the agreement are properly adjudicated in arbitration.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Andrew Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Vicki Gross, CSR #5525, vgross@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/AC)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
